'''Artuo Pratuhr''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] who was the mentor of his assistant [[Zey Nep]], and a patron of the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the final [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He contacted the [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] [[Seib Nod]], interested in selling her stolen relics to the [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]] on Coruscant. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Pratuhr, Nep, and Nod met at the Outlander Club, though Nod did not plan to part with her relics and pulled a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on the archaeologist. Pratuhr tracked down Nod with the help of Nep, took the thief to the authorities, and returned the relics to the Sisterhood.

==Biography==
===Archaeologist and clubgoer===
[[File:Outlander Club.png|thumb|right|350px|Artuo Pratuhr was a patron of the Outlander Club (pictured).]]
Artuo Pratuhr<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|75|Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night}}</ref> was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="AoTC" /> [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]]<ref name="sn">{{DB|character|seibnod|Nod, Seib}}</ref> and, during the final [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], a patron of the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a [[Nightclub/Legends|nightclub]] and [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] hall located in the [[Uscru Entertainment District/Legends|Uscru Entertainment District]] of<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|II|431|Outlander Club}}</ref> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Complete Locations">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="CSWE2" /> Pratuhr<ref name="CSWE" /> was the mentor<ref name="zn">{{DB|character|zeynep|Nep, Zey}}</ref> of his<ref name="CSWE" /> assistant<ref name="sn" /> [[Zey Nep]],<ref name="zn" /> and he was associated with<ref name="CSWE" /> Nep's friends<ref name="zn" /> [[Dixon Just]] and [[Civ Sila]].<ref name="CSWE" /> At some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="SN Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. [[Seib Nod|Seib Nod's]] escape from the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] as shown in her [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry must happen after the altercation between [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] as both Nod and Artuo Pratuhr are both in the club at that time.</ref> [[Seib Nod]], a [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] former member of the religious group [[Sisterhood of the Beatific Countenance]], came to Coruscant inquiring about selling her relics she stole from the Sisterhood.<ref name="sn" /> While on the planet,<ref name="CSWE3" /> Pratuhr contacted her, interested in buying the relics off of Nod to sell to the [[Galactic Museum/Legends|Galactic Museum]]<ref name="sn" /> in Coruscant's [[CoCo Town/Legends|CoCo Town]].<ref name="Museum">{{CSWECite|I|310|Galactic Museum}}</ref>

In 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="NEC Note">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. The scene from ''[[Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter]]'' in which [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] is in the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] takes place immediately before the film, therefore meaning it must be set in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref> Pratuhr, his assistant, and Civ Sila visited the Outlander Club. While they visited, the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Zam Wesell|Zam Wesell]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] were also visiting, making preparations to assassinate [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]].<ref name="Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter">''[[Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> During a [[Local day/Legends|night]]<ref name="AoTC" /> shortly after,<ref name="NEC Note" /> Pratuhr, Nep, and Sila visited the Outlander Club again, this [[Time/Legends|time]] with Dixon Just. Wesell again revisited, trying to escape from the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] after her [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|assassination attempt]] on Amidala. After the Jedi entered, Pratuhr and Nep walked behind the pair. The archaeologist and his associates then talked next to a<ref name="AoTC" /> [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]]<ref name="Complete Locations" /> as Kenobi was at the bar. Just moments after, Wesell was disarmed by Kenobi when she tried to ambush him. Pratuhr's group stared at the Jedi as they helped the injured bounty hunter up. Skywalker then told the clubgoers to go back to their drinks.<ref name="AoTC" />

===Relics robbed===
[[File:SeibNodHS-FFp20.png|thumb|left|200px|Seib Nod (pictured) committed armed robbery against Artuo Pratuhr.]]
After the altercation,<ref name="SN Date" /> Pratuhr and Nep met with Nod in an alcove of the club. Though he was suspicious, Nod assured the archaeologist that the sale was permitted by the Sisterhood in an attempt to quietly raise funds. In actuality, the criminality of Coruscant had changed her, and she pulled a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on Pratuhr and stole his money before escaping the club. Not wanting to lose his money, Pratuhr used his underground connections to locate Nod as she attempted to flee Coruscant.<ref name="sn" /> 

A few days later he located Nod<ref name="CSWE3">{{CSWECite|II|386|Nod, Seib}}</ref> racing unarmed to the dock she was using to leave, recognizing her from her [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] and facial [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]]. Nod thought he was foolish for coming unarmed, but she knew she was beaten when Nep, disguised as a refugee, jabbed a blaster in her back. The archaeologist turned the [[Gender/Legends|woman]] over to the authorities<ref name="sn" /> who arrived at the scene shortly after, landing her charges of theft and armed robbery,<ref name="CSWE3" /> and imprisonment at the [[CoCo Penitentiary]]. Pratuhr turned the relics back to the Sisterhood.<ref name="sn" />

==Personality and traits==
Artuo Prathur was an adventurous archaeologist<ref name="sn" /> with [[Color/Legends|light]] skin and brown hair that grayed<ref name="AoTC" /> by 22 BBY.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Pratuhr had suspicions of the Sisterhood relics stolen by Seib Nod.  When he finally had the relics, he returned them to their rightful owners.<ref name="sn" /> 

==Equipment==
While at the Outlander Club, Pratuhr wore a dark outift,<ref name="AoTC" /> that appeared similar to a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer's]] outfit.<ref name="Insider" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Don has been with Lucasfilm since 1997 and is the most experienced R2-D2 operator in the world – hence the character's name.|Daniel Wallace|<ref name="Insider" />}}
[[File:Bountyhunterss.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Artuo Pratuhr first appeared in ''Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter''.]]
Artuo Pratuhr appeared in the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']],<ref name="AoTC" /> portrayed by Droid Unit supervisor<ref name="AoTC" /> [[Don Bies]],<ref name="Insider" /> in the Outlander Club scenes shot at [[Fox Studios, Sydney]] on [[July 17]], [[2000]].<ref name="Mythmaking">''[[Mythmaking: Behind the Scenes of Attack of the Clones]]''</ref> Prior to the film's release, Pratuhr was first pictured in the documentary "[[Making Episode II Webdocs|A Twinkle Beyond Pluto]],"<ref name="ATBP">{{SW|url=video/a-twinkle-beyond-pluto|series=Making Episode II Webdocs|text=Part 5: A Twinkle Beyond Pluto}}</ref> released on [[July 31]], [[2001]],<ref name="ATBP Date">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/bts/me2/archive.html|text=''Making Episode II Webdocs'' archive}}</ref> The character would go on to make his first appearance in an illustration in ''[[Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter]]'', a young readers book written by [[Eric Arnold]] and illustrated by [[Valerie Reckert]]<ref name="Jango Fett: Bounty Hunter" /> that was published on [[April 23]], [[2002]].<ref name="Amazon">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/young/news20020225.html|text=Book Preview: Episode II Children's Books}}</ref> Episode II later released on [[May 16]], 2002.<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]''</ref> Pratuhr was first identified on<ref name="Insider" /> [[April 6]], [[2004]],<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20040315.html|text=The New Face of Evil}}</ref> with the publication of "[[Who's Who in the Outlander Club: It's Ladies' Night]]," an article written by [[Daniel Wallace]] in the [[Star Wars Insider 75|seventy-fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''. Pratuhr's name comes from "[[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] operator."<ref name="Insider" />
